short: The broad lineup of general and specialized models built by Mistral AI.
definition: Mistral Models refers collectively to Mistral AI's lineup of general and specialized language models, which spans open-weight releases (free to download) and proprietary, paid offerings. The family includes small conventional models, larger ones that route work to specialist sub-networks, versions that also handle images, and models tuned for tasks such as coding. Together they cover both efficiency-focused open releases for the community and higher-capability commercial models for business use.
